title: camelina hispida var. grandiflora
intro: Camelina hispida var. grandiflora, also known as largeflower false flax or wild flax, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Camelina hispida var. grandiflora include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a food source, particularly for its edible seeds.
